content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. You want all the elements of the sequence to be equal. In order to achieve that, you may perform zero or more moves. In each move, you must choose an index iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N), then choose j=i−1j = i-1j=i−1 or j=i+1j = i+1j=i+1 (it is not allowed to choose j=0j = 0j=0 or j=N+1j = N+1j=N+1) and change the value of AiA_iAi​ to AjA_jAj​ — in other words, you should replace the value of one element of the sequence by one of its adjacent elements.

What is the minimum number of moves you need to make in order to make all the elements of the sequence equal?
content.inputFormat:
First line contains an integer T. Each test case contains an integer N, followed by N space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, 
…, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the minimum required number of moves.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: No moves are needed since all the elements are already equal.
Example case 3: We can perform one move on either A1A_1A1​ or A2A_2A2​.

Explanation:
Example case 1: We can perform two moves to make all elements equal to 1.
Example case 2: No moves are needed since all the elements are already equal.

Explanation:
Example case: We can perform four moves to make all elements equal to 1 or 4.

solution.approach: For each test case, we will iterate through the array and for each element, we will find the maximum and minimum value in the subarray from the current element to the end of the array. We will then calculate the minimum number of moves required to make all elements in the subarray equal to the maximum or minimum value. We will repeat this process for all elements in the array and return the minimum number of moves required.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^2) where N is the number of elements in the array, as we are iterating through the array and for each element, we are finding the maximum and minimum value in the subarray.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) as we are only using a constant amount of space to store the maximum and minimum values.

code.Python3:
import sys

T = int(input())

for _ in range(T):
    N = int(input())
    A = list(map(int, input().split()))
    moves = 0

    for i in range(N):
        max_val = A[i]
        min_val = A[i]

        for j in range(i, N):
            max_val = max(max_val, A[j])
            min_val = min(min_val, A[j])

        moves += min(min(i, N - i - 1), min(abs(A[i] - max_val), abs(A[i] - min_val)))

    print(moves)
